content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of decisions made by the Security Committee up to August 29, 2082 (Bhadra 13).

Based on the letter from the District Police Office, Bara dated 2082/05/18, Simara Airport security is currently provided by 15 personnel under a Sub-Inspector. Considering the increase in physical structures and passenger traffic, and to meet international security standards, the existing personnel are deemed insufficient. It is decided to direct the District Police Office to provide an additional 12 personnel.
The current peace and security situation of the district was felt to be normal.
Reviewed the implementation status of decisions made by the Security Committee up to September 7, 2082 (Bhadra 22).

Due to attempted attacks on government property, officials, and private assets during Gen Z protests, a curfew order is issued per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement and assembly are prohibited within the following boundaries of Kalaiya Sub-Metropolitan City and designated areas of Jitpursimara Sub-Metropolitan City from 2:00 PM to 8:00 PM today (2082/05/24).
East: Pasaha River
West: Gandak Canal
North: Badaharwa Chowk
South: Up to Durga Secondary School
Up to 200 meters on both sides of the highway along the Gandak Canal to Pathlaiya road section.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made on the morning of 2082/05/24.

As attacks on government and private property are possible, a district-wide curfew is issued from 10:00 PM tonight (2082/05/24) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/25) under Sections 6(a) and 6b(i). Due to difficulties in controlling the situation, it is decided to request military mobilization from the Ripumardan Battalion, Barahmpuri Barracks.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made up to 2082/05/24.
A district-wide curfew is again issued from 6:00 AM today (2082/05/25) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/26) under Section 6(a) and (b) to prevent attacks on property and personnel during protests.

Considering the critical national situation, it is decided to form a Joint Operation Center (JOC) led by the Chief District Officer and including all security heads to deploy personnel in an integrated manner.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made up to 2082/05/25.
Decided to immediately designate prohibited areas and deploy integrated security personnel if any threat arises.
Decided to arrest and prosecute anyone taking advantage of the situation to cause damage, loot, or conduct violent attacks. Nightly curfews will be issued for several days as needed.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.
Reviewed decisions from the morning of 2082/05/26.
A district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight (2082/05/26) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/27) to prevent looting, arson, and violent attacks.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 2:30 PM on 2082/05/26.
A prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) is issued for Kalaiya Sub-Metropolitan City from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM today (2082/05/27) under Section 6(3a).

A district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight (2082/05/27) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/28) under Sections 6(a) and 6b(i). Military assistance from the Ripumardan Battalion is requested due to the difficulty in maintaining control.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 6:00 AM on 2082/05/28.
Since the abnormal situation resulting from protests has normalized, it is decided to lift all curfews and prohibitory orders after 6:00 AM on 2082/05/29.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.
Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 10:00 AM on 2082/05/27.

Following the dissolution of the House of Representatives by the President on Friday, 2082/05/27, and considering potential illegal assemblies or violence, a prohibitory order is issued for the entire district from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM today (2082/05/28). Additionally, a district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/29). Military assistance from the Ripumardan Battalion is requested per Section 6(2) and 6b(i).
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: Based on security sensitivity analysis, a curfew order is issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, effective from 9:00 PM tonight (2082/05/23) to 9:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/24) within the following designated areas of Birgunj Metropolitan City. Movement and assembly are prohibited.

content.content: East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
North: Gandak Chowk
South: Shankaracharya Gate
Reviewed the implementation status of previous District Security Committee decisions. The overall peace and security situation of the district was analyzed as normal.

In light of recent developments, district security agencies shall analyze security sensitivities, exchange information coordinately, and ensure effective and error-free management of the district's overall peace and security.

Decided to instruct government offices in the district to mandate guard rotations, restrict the use of government vehicles to essential business only, and ensure the protection of public property and office premises.

Reliable security arrangements shall be made at markets, public entities, political party offices, residences of VIPs, and public installations, considering security sensitivities. Nepal Police and Armed Police Force Nepal shall conduct joint and coordinated patrols and security deployments.

In case of crowds where damage to public property or risk to the lives of security personnel/individuals is likely, security forces shall first attempt persuasion. If unsuccessful, crowd control shall be managed progressively through baton charges, water cannons, and warning shots. If the crowd remains uncontained, a systematic (sequential) use of force shall be applied.

A curfew was previously issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, within certain areas of Birgunj Metropolitan City, Parsa district, from 9:00 PM on 2082/05/23 to 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24. It has been decided to extend the duration of this curfew today, 2082/05/24, from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM within the following designated boundaries. All security agencies are directed to implement this order.
East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
South: Shankaracharya Gate
North: Gandak Chowk

content: Following the curfew issued for Birgunj Metropolitan City from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM on 2082/05/24, it has been decided to further extend the curfew within the following boundaries until further notice. All security agencies shall ensure the implementation of this order.

content.content: East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
North: Gandak Chowk
South: Shankaracharya Gate

Reviewed the overall peace and security situation and contemporary security issues. Security arrangements shall be further strengthened through coordinated information exchange among security agencies.

During the 'Gen Z' protests, significant damage occurred to private and public property within Birgunj Metropolitan City due to arson, stone-pelting, and unruly activities. As the situation suggests a high risk of continued unrest, a curfew remains in place. However, as the current situation is difficult for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force Nepal to control alone, it has been decided to request the mobilization of the Nepalese Army (Shreejung Battalion, Parsa) under Section 6(b)(i) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

In the context of protests by Gen Z youth groups across the country regarding the social media ban, all security agencies shall maintain high alert and conduct close monitoring to prevent any undesirable activities within this district.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.
Reviewed previous decisions.
Discussed the situation arising after the Gen Z protests in Kathmandu Valley and other locations on 2082/05/23, and its impact on district security.

Given the situation following the Gen Z protests, it is deemed insufficient for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force alone to prevent unrest at the District Administration Office, other government offices, local municipalities, radio stations, cable cars, and hydropower plants. Therefore, assistance from the Nepalese Army (Shree Garuddal Battalion, Charikot) is requested under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

If peace cannot be maintained through the use of force at government structures mentioned in Decision No. 3 under Section 6(1)(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, the Chief District Officer's order is sought to use firearms aimed below the knee to restore peace, as per Section 6(1)(b) of the same Act.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.

Analyzing the aftermath of the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/23, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) is issued for the entire Dolakha district effective from 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, prohibiting any form of assembly, procession, demonstration, meeting, or picketing under Section 6(3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.
To address security challenges following the Gen Z protests and ensure coordinated management and reporting, a Joint Operation Center (JOC) is established as follows:
